title: Chicken and Dumplings
story: The dish that ends every argument at the table. Slow, simple, and worth every minute.
steps: Put the chicken, carrots, celery, and onion in a large pot and cover with the broth.
Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for about 1 hour, until the chicken is cooked through.
Lift the chicken out, let it cool a little, and pull the meat off the bones in big pieces.
Stir the flour, baking powder, and salt together; add the milk and melted butter to make a soft dough.
Bring the pot back to a gentle simmer and return the chicken to it.
Drop the dough in by big spoonfuls, cover the pot, and cook for 15 minutes without peeking.
Season with pepper and serve hot.
notes: Don't lift the lid while the dumplings cook — the steam is what makes them fluffy.
